assertions.description: DBSC is gated to secure contexts; the page must be HTTPS or localhost.
DBSC piggybacks on fetch — Secure-Session-Registration arrives on a fetch response and the refresh request is a fetch.
DBSC is intentionally invisible to JavaScript — there is no navigator.deviceBoundSessions object. Sites cannot enumerate or forge bindings.
Headers can carry the Sec-Secure-Session-Id token; userland can observe response headers in principle though DBSC headers are filtered.
fetch still accepts credentials: 'include' — DBSC binds the session but does not remove the cookie-credentialed fetch mode.
DBSC relies on per-device key pairs; SubtleCrypto is the user-agent surface used internally and remains exposed to userland.
